target.name: Atomic Industries
target.founder: Aaron Slodov, Austin Bishop, Lou Young Jr

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Atomic Industries is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Aaron Slodov, Austin Bishop, and Lou Young Jr., having raised $25M+. The valuation exercise draws on a broader comp universe of 9 private and 1 public robotics and manufacturing peers, including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity, though narrow peer matching proved constrained.

A single valuation method proved applicable: stage-progression analysis. Using the $25M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression to the next stage, this method yields an implied valuation of $50M. The peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the robotics and manufacturing sector meeting minimum datapoint thresholds.

The triangulated analysis, derived from the single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $42M (low) to $57M (high), with a median point of $50M. Key uncertainties include the limited comp set available at this stage and the reliance on a single methodological input, which constrains confidence in range tightness. A more granular valuation would benefit from operating metrics (revenue run rate, customer concentration, burn profile) and clarity on whether the $25M figure represents post-money valuation or capital deployed.
